title: [PrettifyVerilog] Dead wires can change how the `not` operation is printed
body:
```
circuit Foo:
  module Foo:
    input i: UInt<1>
    output o : UInt<1>
    wire w: UInt<1>
    w <= UInt<1>(1)
    o <= not(i)
```
I would have liked to see `assign o = ~i` in the output:
```
module Foo(	// ./names.fir:2:10
  input  i,
  output o);

  wire w;	// ./names.fir:5:5

  assign w = 1'h1;	// ./names.fir:5:5, :6:10
  assign o = i ^ w;	// ./names.fir:2:10, :5:5, :7:10
endmodule
```
